changes.value: Add task.moveToTop context menu command — move task to top of column

changes.forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,63 +1,35 @@
 ## What

-Add a context menu command `task.moveToTop` that moves the focused task to the top of its current column. The backend `MoveTask` operation already supports `before_id` placement — this command needs to find the first task in the column and set `before_id` to it.
+The "Do This Next" command (right-click on a task card to promote it to top of todo column) exists in the frontend code but doesn't appear in context menus. The code is present in `column-view.tsx:170-193` (added in commit `8661d398`), but the context menu system was migrated to be **backend-driven** — native context menus are built by `list_commands_for_scope` on the Rust side. Frontend-only `extraCommands` with `contextMenu: true` are added to the `CommandScope` but never appear in the native menu.

-### Current state
-- `MoveTask` in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/task/mv.rs` supports `before_id: Option<TaskId>` for placement
-- Entity commands in `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/entity.yaml` define context menu commands with `scope: "entity:task"` and `context_menu: true`
-- Command implementations in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/task_commands.rs` resolve task/column from scope chain
+### Root cause
+- `column-view.tsx:176` defines `{ id: "task.doThisNext", name: "Do This Next", contextMenu: true, execute: () => ... }`
+- This is a frontend-only `CommandDef` passed via `extraCommands` to `useEntityCommands`
+- Context menus are built by `kanban-app/src/commands.rs` → `list_commands_for_scope` which only knows about backend-registered commands
+- `show_context_menu` in the Rust layer calls `list_commands_for_scope` — it doesn't see frontend-only commands

-### Files to modify
-- `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/entity.yaml` — add `task.moveToTop` command definition with `scope: "entity:task"`, `context_menu: true`, `undoable: true`
-- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/task_commands.rs` — add `MoveToTopCmd` implementation that reads the task's current column, finds the first task in that column, and dispatches `MoveTask` with `before_id` set to that first task
-- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/mod.rs` — register `task.moveToTop` in command map
+### Fix approach
+Either:
+**A) Move to backend**: Register `task.doThisNext` as a proper backend command in `entity.yaml` + implement `DoThisNextCmd` in `task_commands.rs`. It dispatches `task.move` with `before_id` set to the first task in todo column. This is the right approach — aligns with the backend-driven command architecture.

-### Implementation
-```rust
-pub struct MoveToTopCmd;
+**B) Hybrid**: Keep the frontend execute callback but also register the command ID in the backend so `list_commands_for_scope` includes it. The backend marks it available when a task is in scope.
+
+Option A is cleaner.

-impl Command for MoveToTopCmd {
-    fn available(&self, ctx: &CommandContext) -> bool {
-        ctx.has_in_scope("task")
-    }
-    
-    async fn execute(&self, ctx: &CommandContext) -> Result<Value> {
-        let kanban = ctx.require_extension::<KanbanContext>()?;
-        let task_id = ctx.resolve_entity_id("task").ok_or(...)?;
-        
-        // Read task to get current column
-        let ectx = kanban.entity_context().await?;
-        let task = ectx.read("task", &task_id).await?;
-        let column = task.get_str("position_column").unwrap_or("");
-        
-        // Find first task in column (lowest ordinal)
-        let tasks = ectx.list("task").await?;
-        let first_in_column = tasks.iter()
-            .filter(|t| t.get_str("position_column").unwrap_or("") == column)
-            .min_by_key(|t| t.get_str("position_ordinal").unwrap_or(""));
-        
-        if let Some(first) = first_in_column {
-            if first.id != task_id {
-                let op = MoveTask::new(task_id, column).with_before_id(first.id.clone());
-                run_op(&op, &kanban).await
-            } else {
-                Ok(json!({ "already_at_top": true }))
-            }
-        } else {
-            Ok(json!({ "no_tasks_in_column": true }))
-        }
-    }
-}
-```
+### Files to modify
+- `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/entity.yaml` — add `task.doThisNext` command def with `scope: "entity:task"`, `context_menu: true`, `undoable: true`
+- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/task_commands.rs` — add `DoThisNextCmd` that reads task's column, finds first task in todo, dispatches `MoveTask` with `before_id`
+- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/mod.rs` — register `task.doThisNext`
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/column-view.tsx` — remove frontend-only `buildDoThisNextCommand` and `taskExtraCommands` (now handled by backend)

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `task.moveToTop` appears in right-click context menu on tasks
-- [ ] Clicking it moves the task to the top of its current column
-- [ ] Task already at top → no-op (no error)
+- [ ] "Do This Next" appears in right-click context menu on task cards
+- [ ] Clicking it moves the task to the top of the todo column
+- [ ] Works from command palette when task is focused
 - [ ] Undoable via Cmd+Z
-- [ ] Appears in command palette when task is focused
+- [ ] Frontend `extraCommands` workaround removed from column-view.tsx

 ## Tests
-- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/mod.rs` — test: `task.moveToTop` available with task in scope, not available without
-- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/dispatch.rs` — integration test: create 3 tasks, move last to top, verify ordinal is lowest
+- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/mod.rs` — test: `task.doThisNext` available with task in scope
+- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/dispatch.rs` — integration test: 3 tasks, doThisNext on last → moves to top of todo
 - [ ] `cargo nextest run -E 'rdeps(swissarmyhammer-kanban)'` — all pass
\ No newline at end of file
changes.reverse_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,35 +1,63 @@
 ## What

-The "Do This Next" command (right-click on a task card to promote it to top of todo column) exists in the frontend code but doesn't appear in context menus. The code is present in `column-view.tsx:170-193` (added in commit `8661d398`), but the context menu system was migrated to be **backend-driven** — native context menus are built by `list_commands_for_scope` on the Rust side. Frontend-only `extraCommands` with `contextMenu: true` are added to the `CommandScope` but never appear in the native menu.
+Add a context menu command `task.moveToTop` that moves the focused task to the top of its current column. The backend `MoveTask` operation already supports `before_id` placement — this command needs to find the first task in the column and set `before_id` to it.

-### Root cause
-- `column-view.tsx:176` defines `{ id: "task.doThisNext", name: "Do This Next", contextMenu: true, execute: () => ... }`
-- This is a frontend-only `CommandDef` passed via `extraCommands` to `useEntityCommands`
-- Context menus are built by `kanban-app/src/commands.rs` → `list_commands_for_scope` which only knows about backend-registered commands
-- `show_context_menu` in the Rust layer calls `list_commands_for_scope` — it doesn't see frontend-only commands
+### Current state
+- `MoveTask` in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/task/mv.rs` supports `before_id: Option<TaskId>` for placement
+- Entity commands in `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/entity.yaml` define context menu commands with `scope: "entity:task"` and `context_menu: true`
+- Command implementations in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/task_commands.rs` resolve task/column from scope chain

-### Fix approach
-Either:
-**A) Move to backend**: Register `task.doThisNext` as a proper backend command in `entity.yaml` + implement `DoThisNextCmd` in `task_commands.rs`. It dispatches `task.move` with `before_id` set to the first task in todo column. This is the right approach — aligns with the backend-driven command architecture.
+### Files to modify
+- `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/entity.yaml` — add `task.moveToTop` command definition with `scope: "entity:task"`, `context_menu: true`, `undoable: true`
+- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/task_commands.rs` — add `MoveToTopCmd` implementation that reads the task's current column, finds the first task in that column, and dispatches `MoveTask` with `before_id` set to that first task
+- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/mod.rs` — register `task.moveToTop` in command map

-**B) Hybrid**: Keep the frontend execute callback but also register the command ID in the backend so `list_commands_for_scope` includes it. The backend marks it available when a task is in scope.
-
-Option A is cleaner.
+### Implementation
+```rust
+pub struct MoveToTopCmd;

-### Files to modify
-- `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/entity.yaml` — add `task.doThisNext` command def with `scope: "entity:task"`, `context_menu: true`, `undoable: true`
-- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/task_commands.rs` — add `DoThisNextCmd` that reads task's column, finds first task in todo, dispatches `MoveTask` with `before_id`
-- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/mod.rs` — register `task.doThisNext`
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/column-view.tsx` — remove frontend-only `buildDoThisNextCommand` and `taskExtraCommands` (now handled by backend)
+impl Command for MoveToTopCmd {
+    fn available(&self, ctx: &CommandContext) -> bool {
+        ctx.has_in_scope("task")
+    }
+    
+    async fn execute(&self, ctx: &CommandContext) -> Result<Value> {
+        let kanban = ctx.require_extension::<KanbanContext>()?;
+        let task_id = ctx.resolve_entity_id("task").ok_or(...)?;
+        
+        // Read task to get current column
+        let ectx = kanban.entity_context().await?;
+        let task = ectx.read("task", &task_id).await?;
+        let column = task.get_str("position_column").unwrap_or("");
+        
+        // Find first task in column (lowest ordinal)
+        let tasks = ectx.list("task").await?;
+        let first_in_column = tasks.iter()
+            .filter(|t| t.get_str("position_column").unwrap_or("") == column)
+            .min_by_key(|t| t.get_str("position_ordinal").unwrap_or(""));
+        
+        if let Some(first) = first_in_column {
+            if first.id != task_id {
+                let op = MoveTask::new(task_id, column).with_before_id(first.id.clone());
+                run_op(&op, &kanban).await
+            } else {
+                Ok(json!({ "already_at_top": true }))
+            }
+        } else {
+            Ok(json!({ "no_tasks_in_column": true }))
+        }
+    }
+}
+```

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] "Do This Next" appears in right-click context menu on task cards
-- [ ] Clicking it moves the task to the top of the todo column
-- [ ] Works from command palette when task is focused
+- [ ] `task.moveToTop` appears in right-click context menu on tasks
+- [ ] Clicking it moves the task to the top of its current column
+- [ ] Task already at top → no-op (no error)
 - [ ] Undoable via Cmd+Z
-- [ ] Frontend `extraCommands` workaround removed from column-view.tsx
+- [ ] Appears in command palette when task is focused

 ## Tests
-- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/mod.rs` — test: `task.doThisNext` available with task in scope
-- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/dispatch.rs` — integration test: 3 tasks, doThisNext on last → moves to top of todo
+- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/mod.rs` — test: `task.moveToTop` available with task in scope, not available without
+- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/dispatch.rs` — integration test: create 3 tasks, move last to top, verify ordinal is lowest
 - [ ] `cargo nextest run -E 'rdeps(swissarmyhammer-kanban)'` — all pass
\ No newline at end of file
